channel.title: Reverb — New Bass Guitars
channel.description: Recently listed bass guitars on Reverb

item.title: 2013 Fender Japan MIJ JB62 '62 Jazz Bass All Black Matching Headstock — $1,299
item.description:
$1,299 · Very Good · Guitar Chimp

For Sale: 2013 Fender Japan MIJ JB62 '62 Jazz Bass All Black w/ Gig Bag

Up for sale is a 2013 Fender Made in Japan JB62 '62 Jazz Bass "All Black", complete with a gig bag.

The All Black JB62 is one of Fender Japan's coolest and most distinctive Jazz Bass models. Based on the classic 1962 Jazz Bass reissue, this limited-production variant takes the vintage design in a stealthy modern direction with an all-black finish, matching black headstock, black-finished neck, black pickguard, and smoked chrome hardware. The result is an incredibly sleek and understated look that sets it apart from virtually every other Jazz Bass Fender has produced.

As with many Fender Japan special models, these rarely appear on the market and have become increasingly desirable among collectors and players alike.

Condition

Very Good – 8.5/10

  • Light surface scratches
  • A few small dings from normal use
  • Minor clear coat discoloration on the back of the neck
  • Clear coat finish cracking around the neck plate on the back of the body

The clear coat discoloration on the neck is cosmetic only and cannot be felt whatsoever. Likewise, the finish cracking around the neck plate is confined to the clear coat and is purely cosmetic, with no impact on the structural integrity or playability of the bass.

Please review all photos carefully for the most accurate representation of condition.

The bass is all original with no cracks, breaks, repairs, or structural issues.

The neck is straight, the original frets are in excellent condition with minimal wear, and the bass has been professionally set up by our in-house luthier. It plays cleanly up and down the neck with no buzzing or dead spots.

A gig bag is included.

Specifications

  • Model: Fender Japan JB62 '62 Jazz Bass All Black
  • Year: 2013
  • Made in Japan (MIJ)
  • Serial Number: JD13000490
  • Finish: All Black
  • Body: Basswood
  • Neck: Maple (Black Finish)
  • Neck Profile: Slim '60s Oval
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood
  • Radius: 7.25"
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • Frets: 20 Vintage-Style
  • Nut Width: 1.52"
  • Neck Depth: .85" at the 1st Fret
  • Neck Depth: 1.00" at the 12th Fret
  • Pickups: Original Fender Japan JB Single-Coils
  • Controls: Volume, Volume, Master Tone
  • Bridge: Vintage-Style 4-Saddle
  • Tuners: Vintage Reverse Gear
  • Hardware: Smoked Chrome
  • Weight: 8 lbs 4 oz
  • Case: Gig Bag

Electronics & Features

The JB62 stays true to the classic passive Jazz Bass formula with a pair of Fender Japan single-coil pickups that deliver the articulate, punchy tone that has made the Jazz Bass one of the most recorded basses in history.

The real standout, however, is the exclusive All Black aesthetic. Fender didn't simply finish the body in black—they carried the theme throughout the entire instrument with a matching black headstock, black-painted neck, black pickguard, and smoked chrome hardware. It's one of the cleanest and most aggressive-looking Jazz Basses Fender Japan has ever produced.

Combined with the vintage-correct 7.25" radius and slim '60s neck profile, it offers classic feel with a truly unique visual identity.

Tone & Feel

This bass delivers everything players expect from a great Jazz Bass—tight lows, articulate mids, and crisp, clear highs with the unmistakable Jazz Bass growl.

The neck pickup produces warm, full-bodied vintage tones, while the bridge pickup adds focused punch and bite. Blending the two delivers the classic Jazz Bass sound heard on countless recordings across every genre.

The slim '60s neck feels fast and comfortable, while the lightweight 8 lb 4 oz body makes this an exceptionally comfortable bass for long rehearsals and performances.

Transparency & Photos

As with all Guitar Chimp listings, detailed photos are provided of the exact instrument—no stock photos.

At the end of the photo set, you'll find close-up images taken under direct lighting to highlight any cosmetic wear.

We don't just describe the condition—we show it in detail so there are no surprises.

Many customers tell us our instruments often arrive in better condition than expected.

Why Buy From Guitar Chimp?

With over 18 years of experience, Guitar Chimp is trusted by players and collectors worldwide for high-quality, player-ready instruments.

Every bass is thoroughly inspected and professionally set up by our in-house luthier.

We are experts at shipping and have safely shipped thousands of guitars and basses around the world. Every instrument is professionally packed, fully insured, and shipped with signature confirmation.

📞 Call/Text

💬 Message anytime—we're always happy to help!

This 2013 Fender Japan MIJ JB62 '62 Jazz Bass All Black is a rare, all-original example that combines classic vintage Jazz Bass tone with one of the coolest factory finishes Fender Japan has ever produced. If you've been looking for an All Black model, this is an exceptionally clean one.

🎸 Jerald – Guitar Chimp

Providing The Finest Guitars, Basses & Amps Since 2006

item.title: Custom 7 String Electric Bass, Burl Wood Top Body, Maple Neck, 24 Frets Fingerboard Fretboard — $532.99
item.description:
$532.99 · Excellent · Myles Music Shop

size: 43 inch Top material : tree burl body: solid okoume wood Fingerboard: Canada maple neck: Canada Maple frets: 24F finish: matte Color: natural scale length: 864mm

item.title: B.C. Rich Beast - black — $1,550
item.description:
$1,550 · Good · Attic-opolis

Made in Korea

BC Rich Beast Bass NJ Series (neck join = bolt on neck)

this is an amazing example of a well played bass

that has no chips!

the bridge has had the Onyx Black finish worn down from hours of rehearsing and giging...

the second owner has had this bass since 2004...

recording and gigging in the Vancouver BC Canada area...

this 'Beast' has been well coveted, played hard and is chock full of 'Metal Mojo'...

Shipped with Hardshell wood construction Form-Fit Case...

(shown in pics)...
Tracking and Insurance included...


BC Rich has been one of the industry standard guitars in the Rock N' Roll Arena,

and Heavy Metal ever since the 1970s.

That is nothing new to those in the know though.

What is new is the changes that BC Rich has made to ensure that their guitar line up was up to date with the latest advances in construction and performance.

It is these changes that have garnered the respect of such popular groups as Slayer and SlipKnot, both of whom are licensed BC Rich musicians.

Check a BC Rich Beast Bass out for yourself and feel the difference!


Body Material - Chesswood

Body Top - Chesswood

Neck - Bolt-on w/ Fastback® Heel

Neck Material - Hard Maple

Fingerboard - Rosewood

Fretwire - Extra Jumbo

Fret Number - 24

Scale - 34"

Inlay - Big Diamond

Head stock Style - 2+2

Beast Hardware - Cosmo Black

Tuners - Sealed Die cast BC Rich

Pickups - 2 BC Rich Special Design P-Style w/ Active Circuit

Active circuit controls Volume, Blend, Treble Boost, Bass Boost

Bridge Type - 4 String Fixed

The NJ Series models were the top import guitars and Basses,

but were discontinued due to a patent issue.

The line was continued as the "Son of Beast" models, but were slightly different in specs

item.title: Fender Precision Partscaster - PB62 + Road Worn Neck — $975
item.description:
$975 · Good · JC's Used Gear

A great partscaster Fender Precision. Body is a Japan PB62. Neck is a Fender Road Worn 50s maple neck. Pickups are EMG GZR. Tuners are GOTOH GB640 Res-o-lite tuners. 

Body and neck have some scratches and dings. It’s more like a very light relic.

Bass weighs 8.8 lbs. 

Plays fantastic. Everything works great. 

Any questions, please let me know.

item.title: Stradi Symphony 6 string Bass guitar 2023 — $5,107.37
item.description:
$5,107.37 · Excellent · Davin 's Boutique


Absolutely stunning bass in every way. The craftsmanship is impeccable! What a joy to play! I can also include a set of strings if what is on the bass presently is not your thing.




Specs:


Scale length: 34"


Body: Deeply Chambered Wenge


Top: Roasted Flamed Maple


Fingerboard: Ziricote


Neck: Purple heart/Afromosia (carbon fiber reinforced)


Frets: EVO Gold


Side dots: Brass


Tuners: Gotoh GB350


Bridge pieces: Hipshot on stainless steel plates


Pickup: Delano X-tender quad coil


Controls: Volume and tone slide pots, single-hum-single switch (all noise-cancelling)


Case: Custom fitted hardcase made in Italy




The bass will be packed professionally and shipped fully insured via FEdEx.

item.title: G&L Fullerton Deluxe JB 2021 - Jet Black USA American Leo Fender — $1,150
item.description:
$1,150 · Excellent · Mike’s Music

This bass is pure gold… except it is black of course. I will definitely regret selling this, but need the cash for house repairs.

Does not come with a case

item.title: Vintage Fender Precision Bass 1977 w/ maple neck/Olympic White/ HSC — $3,500
item.description:
$3,500 · Very Good · Drew's Gear Bazaar

Vintage 1977 Olympic white American fender precision bass with beautiful yellowing patina, natural relic’d. original pickups were replaced at some point with vintage Dimarzio split P (DP127) pickups which are full and fat. If you want to be heard this is the bass for you. comes with hard shell case

item.title: 2007 MIM Fender Jazz Bass Fralin 5% Overwind — $750
item.description:
$750 · Good · JC's Used Gear

2007 Fender Jazz bass with Fralin 5% overwind pick ups, and a custom funky finish.


I bought this bass about four years ago, never really bonded with jazz basses, but for some reason this neck did connect well with me. While it’s made in Mexico, it is very straight and stable, one of the best jazz necks I’ve ever played, being a p bass player.


The bass weighs 9.4 pounds.Tones are nice and punchy. 1.503” width at the nut. Come with a used but sturdy SKB hardshell case. Also comes with a used black pickguard.

There are a few dings on the body, take a look at the pictures.

It is a very, very fun bass to play given the graphics, and again the neck on this thing is surprisingly good for a Mexican made Fender. I would keep it if my hands were smaller.

item.title: Carvin PB4 Bass Guitar BEAD Tuning 2014 - Sunburst — $999.99
item.description:
$999.99 · Excellent · Ken's Guitars

2014 Carvin PB4 Bass Guitar. Beautiful gloss Sunburst finish and Maple neck. Alder body, stainless steel frets, string through bridge, contoured neck heel, Alnico V pickups. In excellent condition with just some light pickguard scratching from normal use. Nice Birdseye figuring on the fretboard. Currently set up in BEAD tuning but could be easily converted back to traditional set-up. Nice low action with absolutely no fret buzz. Includes the pictured gig bag.

Please ask any questions prior to purchase as this is as pictured/described and is being sold as-is.

item.title: Mike Lull M5V 2012 — $3,770
item.description:
$3,770 · Very Good · Namson's Gear Depot

-W/hard case

-Duncan jazz style pickup

-35” scale length

item.title: Fender Bass VI 1965 — $13,999
item.description:
$13,999 · Very Good · Candy’s Gear Locker

1965 Fender Bass VI – Lake Placid Blue – Vintage – Featured by Chicago Music Exchange

Offered for sale is an exceptional 1965 Fender Bass VI finished in the highly sought-after Lake Placid Blue custom color.

The Bass VI is one of Fender’s most iconic and collectible vintage instruments, combining characteristics of a bass, guitar, and baritone into one incredibly versatile design. Tuned one octave below a standard guitar (E to E), it features a 30” scale length, floating vibrato, three single-coil pickups, individual pickup switches, and Fender’s unique “strangle” switch, allowing for an impressive variety of vintage tones.

This is the same instrument previously sold by Chicago Music Exchange and featured in their YouTube demonstration video, where they discuss the history, features, and playability of this exact Bass VI. The guitar also includes the original Chicago Music Exchange Professional Quality Certification card documenting the instrument when it passed through their shop.

The Bass VI has been played by legendary artists including Carol Kaye, Glen Campbell, George Harrison, John Lennon, Robert Smith (The Cure), and many others. Today it remains one of the most collectible vintage Fender models ever produced.

Please review all photos carefully, as they are part of the description. Additional photos are available upon request.

Included

  • 1965 Fender Bass VI
  • Original hard shell case
  • Chicago Music Exchange Professional Quality Certification card

Condition

This is a vintage instrument and shows honest cosmetic wear consistent with its age. Please examine all photographs carefully to judge cosmetic condition for yourself.

Chicago Music Exchange demonstration video of this exact instrument can be found on YouTube.

item.title: Washburn 5 string Active Bass 1998 Sunburst — $2,845.25
item.description:
$2,845.25 · Excellent · Attic-opolis

*NEW* 1998 Washburn XB925:
Washburn's top of the line solid body USA made 5 string bass...

The XB Series was designed to have great looks... a great feel and a great sound... this bass has a beautiful flame maple top... on a genuine swamp ash body... and 2 piece maple neck with a rosewood fingerboard...
Bartolini pickups and electronics make this model...
Custom ordered NEW and not picked up by the customer... model made in the USA
Red Orange sunburst top
Bartolini active pickups... 9-volt battery...
hardshell moulded 'Chainsaw' style factory case...
Buzz Feiten tuning system... Serial # 9809021
year made = 1998
Master Volume x 1... center notched tone x 2... tone select switch 2 positions... neck and bridge pick-ups mixed with - 1 stacked and notched double potentiometer...
great tone from these advances active electronics...

item.title: USA Fender American Standard Precision Bass - with G&L MFD pickup and Rosewood Fretboard 2008 - 2016 - Black — $999.99
item.description:
$999.99 · Very Good · Mike’s Music

Sweet bass, but I can win for loosing with things breaking around the house and another must go. I will regret selling this, but I just got it and has the least attachment to me. Please help it find a forever home while I foster it.


Does not include a case or original pickup.

item.title: Gibson Grabber G3 70s Reissue 2012 - Black Satin Nitro — $1,650
item.description:
$1,650 · Good · AP

This an excellent Gibson bass, plays and sound great very small dings on the sides, and it has a slightly scratchy switch. Other than those two small things it’s in perfect condition. Message me with any questions. Comes with a Gibson gig bag

item.title: Vox 'White Shadow' 4 string Bass 1986 black — $1,420.25
item.description:
$1,420.25 · Excellent · Attic-opolis

Vox White Shadow Bass...
This bass guitar was built at Matsumoku factory in Japan - a rare example of the Vox take on the P bass configuration...
the neck is a J bass style neck... with a superb feel and taper...
the split P bass style ;pick up... has  tap coil switching for a greater tonal range...
Volume and Tone controls...
the body is well balanced...
a cool body shape if you don't want to be everybody else...
and the 'Thunder Bird' head stock tops off the unique look...
Vox never built their own instruments in the UK... but subcontracted them to several international companies,
for example: Italian Eko in the 60s
for the famous teardrop-shaped Mark VI or the Phantom...
or in the case of the White Shadow to Aria -
in the early 80s for Vox guitars 1980's come-back
shipped in a hard shell bass flight case...

item.title: Fender Standard Precision Bass with Rosewood Fretboard Black w Case — $619.99
item.description:
$619.99 · Excellent · B Major Music

Sounds and plays so good! Straight neck. Fully functional. Slight cosmetic blemishes, nothing major. Still has the sticker on it from factory. A rare find with case!


Don't miss your chance to own!


Items only come with case or accessories if pictured or listed in the listing.

Items only come with accessories/parts pictured and or listed in the listing.

Items are all setup to play each note unless otherwise listed. Items may have to be setup by user for personal preference.


INTERNATIONAL BUYERS!! If the shipping price is very high for you we recommend you using a service such as Shipito or PlanetExpress which will provide you with a USA address and forward on to your country. These are well reviewed and offer great service. Message us if you need assistance. We can usually provide estimated weight and dimensions. Non-Continental USA send us a message.   


Returns are subject to 10% restocking fee. 

Returns will be required to have the original packaging supplies and shipping box. If you destroy or dispose of the packaging or shipping box you forfeit ability to return item for defects or any other reason. If item arrives in different condition than listing buyer must contact us and await further instructions. 


We are committed to excellence in serving you, if you have any questions feel free to message.


Your item will ship within 1-3 business days of payment received. Tracking provided.

Extra shipping outside lower 48 US States.


Thank you!

item.title: 1995 Fender MIJ Foto Flame Precision Bass - Natural - Original Case — $2,795
item.description:
$2,795 · Excellent · Chuck's Gear Outlet

1995 Fender Japan Precision Bass – Natural Foto Flame – Made in Japan – Original Hardshell Case

This is an all-original 1994 Fender Japan Precision Bass (serial S011699) in the Natural Foto Flame finish. It is in excellent cosmetic and playing condition and includes the original Fender hardshell case.

The bass has been well cared for and shows very little wear. The neck is straight, the truss rod functions properly, and the electronics work exactly as they should. Frets have minimal wear.

One unique feature is the well-known “Hamburglar” Foto Flame anomaly visible on the back of the neck and by the fretboard. Collectors of Fender Japan Foto Flame instruments will recognize this unusual manufacturing quirk. I’ve included close-up photos of the pattern.

Features:

  • Made in Japan
  • Serial Number: S011699
  • Natural Foto Flame finish
  • Rosewood fingerboard
  • All original hardware and electronics
  • Original Fender hardshell case
  • Non-smoking home

Please review all photos carefully, as they are part of the description. I’m happy to answer any questions or provide additional photos.

item.title: Peavey Millennium AC BXP 5 string — $325
item.description:
$325 · Very Good · Guitar Gear

5 string Peavey bass for sale. Has VFL pickups similar to the Peavy Cirrus sound. Great shape and has an upgraded preamp with tone control and active/passive options (I left it inside the cavity to prevent drilling).

item.title: DeArmond DLX Pilot 1999 - Natural — $878.75
item.description:
$878.75 · Excellent · Attic-opolis

DeArmond DLX Pilot Neck Thru Bass


4- string Bass.

Ash body, neck-thru,

22 fret,

natural satin finish.

A few scratches on the body,

plays and sounds great!

Active pick-ups

item.title: Gretsch G2220 Junior Jet Bass II with Walnut Fretboard 2018 - Present - Walnut Stain — $205
item.description:
$205 · Good · Andrew's GearStop

Selling my Gretsch G2220 Electromatic Junior Jet Bass in Walnut.

Condition is good overall, with a few dings, scratches, and scuffs from normal use (see photos), so it’s priced accordingly. Everything functions as it should and it plays great.

Bought it last summer with the intention of learning bass, but I never really got around to it, so it’s time for someone else to enjoy it. Feel free to reach out with any questions!


* Local pickup only

item.title: Shawn May Custom Basses DCS5B 5 String Bass Guitar- Aguilar Pickups — $1,950.56
item.description:
$1,950.56 · Excellent · Bassist_

May Bass DCS5B "Rusty Griswold"

Signature

The May Bass DCS5B “Rusty Griswold” Signature is a one-of-a-kind instrument that fuses boutique craftsmanship with raw, industrial character. Built on a classic 34” scale with 24 frets, it delivers both comfort and extended range for modern players.

Its alder body is finished in a striking “Tetanus Shot” patina using Modern Masters rust treatment, sealed with a Target EM6000 flat-in acrylic topcoat for durability without losing its gritty edge. A thin acrylic pickguard—complete with a single cracked screw hole—adds just the right touch of road-worn authenticity.

The 4-piece maple neck with a maple fretboard features an Fe3O2 inlay, tying the instrument’s theme together with subtle, industrial flair. A scalloped unbleached bone nut ensures clarity and precision across the range.

Electronics are all pro-grade: a powerful Aguilar DCBM5 pickup paired with a Bartolini NTMB preamp provides a huge tonal palette, from growling low-end punch to articulate highs. Hardware is equally road-ready, featuring a Hipshot D style bridge, Gotoh Res-O-Lite tuners, and Dunlop recessed strap locks for rock-solid performance.

Despite its rugged appearance, the bass is lightweight at 8.4 lbs, making it a comfortable choice for long sessions or gigs.

The “Rusty Griswold” isn’t just an instrument—it’s a statement piece. Equal parts art, engineering, and attitude, it’s designed for players who want boutique precision wrapped in a raw, industrial aesthetic.

- ﻿﻿34" scale / 24 frets

- ﻿﻿Alder Body in "Tetanus Shot" finish (Modern Masters Rust patina)

- ﻿﻿4pc Maple neck

- ﻿﻿Maple fretboard w/ Fe3O2 inlay

- ﻿﻿Aguilar DCBM5 pickup

- ﻿﻿Bartolini NTMB preamp

- ﻿﻿Hipshot D style bridge

- ﻿﻿Gotoh Res-O-Lite tuners

- ﻿﻿Dunlop recessed strap locks

- ﻿﻿Scalloped unbleached bone nut

- ﻿﻿Target EM6000 "flatin" acrylic topcoat

- ﻿﻿Thin acrylic pickguard with 1 cracked screw hole for that "authentic look"

- ﻿﻿8.4lbs

item.title: Daion Power mark XX-B 1981 -4 string bass guitar (Tan ) vintage — $1,500
item.description:
$1,500 · Good · Lenny's Gear Locker

This has been in storage for years, Besides a knob being missing this bass plays great. There’s flat wounds on it.
All original. Looks like the dots on the fretboard were painted on. Not from me,

Mark XXs from 1981-1983 had black anodized aluminum knobs and switch tips. These knobs and switch tips are not shared with any guitar manufacture outside of Daion – they are unique!
Two pickups in a “P/J” setup – split coil neck and single 8 pole pickup for the bridge. Three way pickup selector. Two volume and two tone knobs.

9-piece Maple and Rosewood through neck construction, two octave (24 fret) Ebony fingerboard with brass position markers and ivory colored celluloid binding.

Color: Tinted Tan (TAN)

TAN was an Ash body with Rosewood lamination


sold as is.

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man Classic StingRay 4 | 2009 Mint Green | Rare Pre-Production — $2,499
item.description:
$2,499 · Very Good · The Burnside

About The Instrument:
This is a very rare and interesting Music Man StingRay Classic from the initial launch of the "Classic" model. Notable quirks include a string-thru body, raw maple neck, old logo, and compensated nut. (As opposed to the eventual final iteration of Classics in 2010 that opted for lacquered necks, thru-bridge, modified logo, and vintage nut). The 2009 models were available in small numbers through Guitar Center and a few dealer one offs.

The bass is in excellent working condition, setup with low action, in standard tuning, with Ernie Ball Cobalt Flats (.045-100s). The mutes are engaged but easy enough to disengage if you prefer. Everything behaves as expected. Cosmetically the most notable blemish is a small dark scratch on the treble side of the neck rear around the 12th fret (photographed). Otherwise there are minor dings, imperfections, and playwear. This thing absolutely thumps and is in my opinion one of the best Rays you can buy. The flats + mutes are great if you want the Ray clarity and articulation without being obnoxious.


Actual Specifications Captured from This Instrument:

  • Serial: B050105 (Dec 14th 2009)
  • Weight: 10lbs 2oz
  • Nut Width: 1.69"
  • Neck Depth @ 1st: 0.84"
  • Neck Depth @ 12th: 0.90"
  • Neck Shape: Typical C
  • Relief: 0.008"
  • Action @ 12th: 0.08"
  • Frets: Excellent condition, could use a polish but no significant wear
  • Strings: Ernie Ball Cobalt Flats .45-100
  • Controls: Master Volume, Bass, Treble, Output, 9V Battery, Individual String Mutes
  • Pickup: Music Man StingRay Classic Humbucker
  • Case: Aftermarket Gator Metro Padded Gig Bag (excellent condition)

About The Shop:

Instruments are stored on wall hangers in a temperature (73 F) and humidity controlled (45-55%) smoke-free environment. Items are typically shipped same or next business day via UPS depending on purchase time. Please ask any and all questions before making a purchase. I want you to be happy, so please meet me on the level. I'm always happy to take additional photos or demo videos.

All instruments are well maintained and cared for, but they are tools that are used. Unless otherwise noted, assume nothing for sale is targeted towards collectors or mint/brand new shoppers.

Local pickup is available and encouraged in the Ocean County New Jersey area

item.title: Vigier Arpege Series II Delta Metal Fretless Bass Dark Cherry El. Bass w/Case — $2,599.99
item.description:
$2,599.99 · Very Good · Direct Music Sales

Up for sale a Vigier Arpege Series II Delta Metal Fretless Bass Dark Cherry Electric Bass w/Case - #052! This beautiful instrument is a Used Model in Very Good Cosmetic Condition and in Perfect Playing Condition. On this one, there are some scratches, marks, few dents, barely few tiny nicks, slight finish/plating deterioration and few other signs of wear here and there (see 11th to 24th pictures), but nothing major. Also, there are tapes, scratches, notches, tolex rips and other signs of wear on the case (see 9th and 10th pictures). Unit comes in a Hardshell Case. 

- 

VIGIER ARPEGE SERIES II DELTA METAL FRETLESS BASS

Vigier Guitars was a French musical instruments company based in Grigny, Essonne and founded by Patrice Vigier in 1980. In addition to manufacturing, the company was also active in the import and wholesale distribution of musical instruments, amplifiers and accessories through its division High Tech Distribution.

Vigier's designs was popular on the Continent and in America. Despite the rock emphasis in the styling of the basses however, they are capable of a very wide range of sounds due in part to their mechanical construction and in part to their versatile active electronics.

The Arpege Active Bass, and the Fretless Bass with Metal Fingerboard are just part of a range developed by Vigier as an independent designer.

-

FEATURES:

- Neck-through construction with a trapezoidal extension

- Metal reinforcement board underneath the fingerboard

- Metal fretboard 

- Active electronics

- Fixed bridge

- Benedetti custom humbuckers- Includes premium molded hardshell case

-

SPECIFICATIONS:

Construction : Neck-through-body design (trapezoidal shape under the body)

Body Material : Walnut

Finish : Hand-applied varnish 

Color : Dark Cherry

Neck Material : Maple / Walnut

Fingerboard : Delta Metal (a proprietary gold-colored alloy)

Frets : Fretless with lines

Nut : Brass

Scale Length : 34"

Pickups : Custom hand-wound Benedetti single coil/twin-coil pickups

Controls : 

- Three-way switch is a conventional rhythm/lead/both pickup selector.

- The central pair of rotary pots operate as follows; the higher knob switches on the parametric EQ if pulled up, and switches it off if pushed down. 

- Rotating the pot boosts or cuts the selected frequency by twenty dB in the on position

- The lower knob determines the frequency at which the parametric EQ operates.

- The two-way switch decides whether the pickups operate in single or double coil mode

- Tthe final knob is an overall tone control, and the side socket is the overall output.

Preamp : Vigier active 18-volt system

Bridge & Tuners : Custom Vigier bridge and Schaller machine heads

Weight : 10.8 lbs / 4.9 kg

-

All our items are fully verified by our pro technician and/or adjusted by our pro luthier before being very carefully packed by our pro service team here at the shop prior to shipment, buy with confidence!

---------------------------------------------------------------------

Canadian customers must pay any applicable sales taxes. 

Please refer to the shipping and payments details for prices.

**Depending on where you are located in Canada, free shipping may not apply to certain heavy or oversize orders.
 You can contact us for confirmation before buying.

Shipping cost to USA includes lower 48 states only.

For any other locations, please send us an email for a quote! 


We Ship Worldwide!!!

To international buyers (outside Canada): Import duties, taxes, and charges are not included in the item price or shipping cost. These charges are the buyer's responsibility. Please check with your country's customs office to determine what these additional costs will be prior to bidding or buying.

item.title: Warwick Vampyre Darklord — $3,800
item.description:
$3,800 · Very Good · Dave's Gear Bazaar

Recently acquired in a trade but I pretty much exclusively play passive bc rich basses (and have been leaning more into guitar again) so my loss is your gain if you know how rare these are


Some light buckle rash on the body and one small tear on the coffin case

item.title: Spector Rex 5 neck-thru with nice upgrades — $1,299
item.description:
$1,299 · Good · Vintagegtrs

Rex 5 flamed body, flamed neck.


Upgraded with emg dc40x

Aguilar opb-3 with stacked mid control

This one is beautiful and thumps hard

I can find maybe 1-2 scratches. This bass belonged to PJ Rubal at some point and was well taken care of i found a few small marks. Where the fretboard connects to the neck, there seems to be a finish repair, it was well done but you can see something there just disclosing everything

”Spector” appears to be stenciled on the case. I’m not sure if it came that way or someone did it but it is a very nice gator hardshell case

item.title: Charvel B3 1987 - Pearl white — $1,075
item.description:
$1,075 · Very Good · Guitars and Gear ! USA

Charvel 3B bass

Made in japan

Neck-thru-body construction

Active pickups

  • Body: Poplar (offset P-style)
  • Neck: Maple (Neck-thru-body construction)
  • Fretboard: Rosewood with pearloid dot inlays (21 Jumbo frets)
  • Scale Length: 34 inches
  • Nut Width: 42.7 mm
    Plays Great
  • Some scratches a d dents as shown on pics!
  • Thanks

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass, Aerodyne, US Gun Metal Blue, MIJ 2016 — $1,050.03
item.description:
$1,050.03 · Very Good · String Switch Noise


2016, Made In Japan, “JD16” serial


Factory UGB – US Gun Metal Blue colour finish


Three ply black pickguard


Offset with beautiful white body binding


Sleek and modern looks


Contoured body top with recessed bridge


Maple one piece neck


Slab Rosewood fretboard


7.25” board, 20 vintage size frets, 34” scale


39mm nut width


Jazz Bass single coil pickup at the bridge


Precision Bass split single-coil pickup in the middle position


Versatile tone from J Bass cleans to the grit and punch of a P Bass


Great overall condition


Includes a Fender Gig Case/Bag, and original serial card paper work




ITEM CONDITION

All machinery, electrical, and pickups are without issue. Some light surface marks and rash, some small impressions/dings/dimples/dents/chips, light aging and tarnish to hardware - as pictured.





POSTAGE & SHIPPING NOTE:

- Upon cleared payment, all small items (pedals, wiring etc) are usually posted within 24-72hr


- Guitars and Basses are packed on Monday (courier collection Tuesday) and Thursday (for courier collection Friday)


Meaning, if your purchase is on a Thursday (after 1pm), Friday, Saturday, Sunday, or Monday (before 1pm) it will be couriered Tuesday.

If your purchase is on Monday (after 1pm), Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday (before 1pm) it will be couriered Friday.


We take great care and pride in making sure guitars are carefully set up and packed correctly for transport, so we do not rush this step.




The String Switch Noise store operates primarily as an online business.

However, we do have a dedicated showroom 30min south of the Brisbane CBD.

By appointment, we are available Monday-Friday 10am-5pm (and Saturday and Sunday by availability) to test out and pick up purchased items.

Please call or contact us directly through the website.

String Switch Noise



---Shipping---

--- International delivery is possible for most items and can be arranged at exact cost of shipping. However, there are some restrictions with regions and package size. Please contact us first BEFORE making any purchase to check if an item can be delivered, and to organise the ACTUAL delivery costs. International shipping pricing (if found on listing) is an estimate only (it may be more, but it also may be less). Any applicable customs, duties, or taxes that your country may charge is solely the responsibility of you the buyer. Any questions you may have about this should be directed to your local countries authorities.

--- We proudly strive to send out all orders within 24 hours of purchase and/or receiving cleared payment (apart from holidays and weekends). However please allow up to 72hrs for packing and courier pickups. Once your item is packed you will receive notification and relevant tracking number and courier/postal details.

--- Small items such as effect pedals and wiring harnesses are sent via Australia Post standard service, with tracking ONLY. Both domestically and internationally. No responsibility will be taken from us for the loss or damage of an item by Australia Post. At your request, signature upon delivery or postal insurance can be added for the additional cost. Please contact us before purchase, or immediately after purchase.

--- Australia wide, guitars are sent via courier delivery with full insurance, tracking, and signature upon delivery services. Guitar packages MAY be sent via Australia Post if the delivery area is in a remote region/outside normal courier delivery areas/outside normal costs (and the package size is under 105cm long).

--- Courier deliveries can NOT be sent to a PO Box, Parcel Locker, or Parcel Collect service. They must be sent to a physical address, where someone can sign for the item on arrival.

--- All items are packed to the highest degree for transit. Proudly, our packaging is at the highest of standards, with the best reputation you will find anywhere in the industry. This comes from 3500+ guitars (and counting) being sent without issue both Australia wide and internationally over 10+ years (and counting). A great amount of pride and detail goes into making sure every item arrives safe and in a timely manner. Pedals and small items are packed in multiple layering of bubble wrap and double corrugated shipping cardboard. Guitars, pending the instrument, have strings de tuned so there is no tension on the neck and headstock. Neck/body /headstock are wrapped separately in multiple bubble wrap layers, high density foam and/or cardboard reinforced. If applicable it is then placed in gig/soft case or hard case. If in gig/soft case it is once again bubble wrapped/foam reinforced before being boxed. Each package/box is custom cut and fit in heavy duty double corrugated shipping cardboard. All voids are padded out and the body edge, base, and top of head stock foam reinforced/padded. Finally, the boxing will have copious amounts of high visibility “Fragile” and “This way up” warning labels.

--- We are also extremely environmentally conscious. We re use and recycle almost all bubble wrap, foam, and cardboard packaging materials.




---INFO & TERMS---

--- We do not accept any trade, trade ins, consignment, or direct customer purchases.

--- All payments to be made in full at time of purchase. The are no holds, deposits, or layby.

--- Payment can be made by Paypal, Credit Card (through Paypal), Bank Deposit/Online Transfer, Afterpay, or ZIP.

--- If payment is made via Paypal goods and service, the item will ONLY be posted/couriered to your registered Paypal address (NO alternative address). This is to ensure the Paypal buyer and seller insurance protection is valid.

--- All photos are our own and are of the actual item being offered for purchase.

We NEVER use stock photos.

An extensive amount of time and effort is put in to accurately show the items true condition, colour, and shape. Though please realise the limitations of photography. Images and colours can be perceived differently depending of the type and quality of the device it is viewed on.

--- Advertised photos form a large part of the representation of the items condition. Please view the photos carefully as well as reading all item descriptions and/or condition text. Whilst every effort is made to photograph all parts of items, not every little dimple/micro scratch can or will be photographed. We always photograph any and all large cosmetic flaws, such as paint chips/cracks/large dents, that we consider to be significant.

--- Cosmetically, items are as per detailed photos and/or descriptions. Functionally, all pots, jacks, switch and machinery components are working without issue, and serviced upon arrival. Each item is tested and/or serviced/set up before listing. It is also tested again moments before packaging, after purchase, to insure it is 100%.

---Guitars and basses are set up with action at a standard with no playability issues, unless otherwise described. However please understand no one set-up is right for everybody. If you prefer a different action height/tuning/string gauge please adjust at your own liking. Please also realise that almost all items are used, and most are 20-30+ year vintage instruments/effects. They will exhibit some wear and tear. If you need detailed specifics, extra information/on the bench specs can be given about anything you require. Please do not hesitate to ask if this is necessary.

--- No refund will be given for change of mind. There will be no warranty or refund given other than dead on arrival, broken, or faulty that is NOT already described and outlined in the descriptive text and detailed photos. In the HIGHLY unlikely event, there is damage in transit, a postal/courier insurance claim will be made to recover the cost of damage. However, we are always happy to help and work through any issue, in the rare event that one arises.

item.title: Rogue Series III Fretless Conversion 5 string bass guitar — $150
item.description:
$150 · Fair · Tove's Axe Shoppe

Rouge III Series modified fretless 5 string bass guitar. Has been used extensively, but well-cared for. Here are the specs:


-45 in scale length

-Rosewood fretboard

-Black hardware

-Hardtail bridge

-Bolt-on neck

-Pearl dot inlays

-Strings have not been changed

-4 knobs, 2 volumes 3 tones interchangeably

-Jazz bass configuration

-Some dents and blemishes and frets have been stripped off but it plays great

-No serial number

-No truss rod cover

-24 frets

-Does not include hard-case or gig bag

item.title: Fender Jazz ( MIM )- tobacco sunburst — $550
item.description:
$550 · Very Good · Kid's Gear Depot

No issues ....plays great sounds great .....comes with nice Road Runner gig bag

item.title: FENDER PRECISION ELECTRIC BASS, ALL ORIGINAL PARTS, W OHSC - 1972 — $4,000
item.description:
$4,000 · Excellent · Loud and Clear Music

A Fender Precision bass from 1972. Made in the USA, this bass is in very good condition for its age. All parts are original and there are no major cosmetic issues on around the body. Includes original hardshell case. 

Body: Maple

Neck: Maple

Fretboard: Maple

Scale Length: 34"

Nut Width: 1.5"

Weight: 8.8Lbs

Electronics: Orignal Fender P-bass pickups

item.title: Fender Road Worn Precision Bass — $975
item.description:
$975 · Very Good · Jay's Gear

mim road worn p bass w labella flat wound strings

item.title: Fender Precision Bass 1978 Black - All Original Electronics - Excellent Condition - Brass Nut & FCGR Saddles — $2,920.91
item.description:
$2,920.91 · Good · SAYUの機材アウトレット

Up for sale is a stunning 1978 Fender Precision Bass in a classic Black finish with a Rosewood fingerboard. This instrument is a prime example of the late-70s P-bass sound—powerful, punchy, and built to last.

Key Features & Condition:

• Electronics: 100% Original. All pickups and pots are period-correct and functioning perfectly.

• Neck & Playability: The neck is in excellent condition with no issues. The truss rod is fully functional with plenty of room to adjust in both directions.

• Frets: Original frets are preserved. They show some wear consistent with age but are in good condition for playing without any buzzing or dead spots.

• Upgrades: Features a Brass Nut and Freedom Custom Guitar Research (FCGR) bridge saddles for enhanced resonance and precise intonation. The original factory saddles are included.

• Weight: 4.9kg (approx. 10.8 lbs). This substantial weight, combined with the brass nut, provides incredible sustain and a tight, focused low-end that cuts through any mix.

Sound Profile:

This bass delivers the quintessential "P-Bass sound." While 60s reissues or Custom Shop models can sometimes feel polite, this '78 model has a raw, aggressive authority. The sustain is exceptionally long, and the tone remains clear and punchy even with the tone rolled off. It’s an instrument that proves the saying: "A great P-bass doesn't even need new strings to sound amazing."

Cosmetics:

In excellent vintage condition. While there are minor surface scratches and buckle rash typical for its age, it has been remarkably well-preserved. Includes the original Fender hard shell case, which is also in great shape.


• Potentiometers: Original CTS pots with 1978 date codes (13778XX).

• Pickups: Original gray-bottom pickups with period-correct stamps.

• Neck Stamp: Features the authentic code 0100-2295, confirming its production era and matching perfectly with the S8-series serial number.

• Condition: The neck pocket and heel are very clean, showing no signs of structural repair or modification.

item.title: Unbranded Precision Bass Late 2025 - Mahogany Polyurethane — $250
item.description:
$250 · Excellent · String Rescue

  • On offer is a custom made (not by me!) precision bass body. The finish is polyurethane gloss. Genuine Fender split coil pickup. standard p-bass electronics with ‘bumblebee’ capacitor. Unique rear access control cavity eliminating the need for a pick guard. All ready to go, just needs a neck!

item.title: ESP Surveyor-5 2004-2007 - Natural — $1,100
item.description:
$1,100 · Very Good · Christopher's Leftorium

Body is 2 piece ash, neck and fingerboard are maple. Carved out neck pickup pocket to allow for a larger pickup. Both pickups are aftermarket Seymour Duncan MM pickups coupled with an aftermarket Audere active preamp system. Controls are Volume, Pan, Treble, Mid (with frequency switch), Bass. Operates on 18v. It is a powerhouse of tone! Original Wilkinson bridge and Gotoh tuning machines, all working as they should. Frets have a lifetime ahead of them. Just took the strings off and wiped it down, tried to get pictures of all finish imperfections. There are a handful but nothing large or major that ever bothered me. I was a little imperfect in enlarging the neck pickup pocket and chipped a little bit of the clear coat around that. The pictures should say it all so take a good look at those and decide if any of the blemishes bother you before purchasing, but nothing is noticeable from 6+ feet away. I have a few basses and I don’t play terribly much so this bass has seen some play but not an excessive amount from myself. New D’Addario XL string set 6/5. I will be open to offers but please don’t insult me with a lowball offer, I have invested a decent amount of money, time and work on this one and it is an incredible sounding bass. No trades. Will come in quality Gator gig case and will be well packed.


I have yet to see another lefty around but obviously they are out there.

item.title: Gibson SG Bass 2005 Cherry — $1,499
item.description:
$1,499 · Good · Bond Street Guitars

A classic. An all timer. The SG bass is one of the most rock and roll instruments in the brief and beautiful history of cranked full stacks, big hair and pretending to be Jack Bruce or Mike Watt or Kim Gordon Or Phil Lesh and so on. All stock, beautiful setup and more mojo than money should be able to buy but through the grace of the gods of rock it's here, in Brooklyn, waiting for you. In a strange turn of events following this divine intervention it's wound up barely scratched up in its time changing hands but with chips taken out of the volume knobs. Don't ask me how that happened I just work here. Either way any issues are purely cosmetic this thing plays like butter and sounds like a heard of elephants ready to blow the subs at your local dive bar.

item.title: Duesenberg Alliance Chris Chaney Bass - Black — $3,289
item.description:
$3,289 · Brand New · Knox Guitars

SOLID-BODY CONSTRUCTION

The Alliance Series Chris Chaney features a solid Alder body with smooth body contours
and a bolt-on long-scale Maple neck with Rosewood fretboard.

PICKUPS

Custom Duesenberg Grand Duplex Bass pickups deliver a clear and transparent tone
while still providing the deep bottom end frequencies required for a bass.

PLEK PROCESSED

A pleked fretboard guarantees perfectly level frets. This is the basis for our fret polishing and setup work all done by hand that eventually makes our instruments play like nothing you have ever played before.

DURING HIS TIME WITH JANE'S ADDICTION AND WHILE CURRENTLY ON TOUR WITH AC/DC, CHRIS CHANEY IS CONTINUOUSLY PROVING TO BE THE DEEP RHYTHM POWER-HOUSE EVERY BAND NEEDS.

This long-scale solid Alder body Alliance Series is packed with innovative features like our new Grand Duplex Bass Pickups and large anodized aluminium pickguard, all while retaining a sleek and elegant package.
Custom laser-etched control knobs and neck plate, as well as custom fretboard inlays at the 12th fret make this a truly unique low-frequency workhorse.

INCLUDED:
DUESENBERG CUSTOM LINE BAG

THE DUESENBERG "CUSTOM LINE BAG" IS THE IDEAL COMPANION FOR THE ACTIVE URBAN MUSICIAN.

Lightweight, yet extremely good at protecting your instrument, this bag has got you covered on the way to your next rehearsal or gig. The internal padding provides high impact protection and the tuck away shoulder straps allow you to use this bag like a case whenever you see fit.

  • Silver Duesenberg brand elements stitching on the front and the shoulder strap
  • Durable, heavy duty materials & high impact padding
  • Black plush lining & external storage pockets for accessories
  • Shoulder straps can be tucked away for use like a case
  • Padded handle and backpack straps
  • Adjustable neck pillow featuring Duesenberg stitching

All items will be shipped with insurance, and most items (typically $100 and up) will require a signature. Please see the photos for exact condition and send us a message with any questions!

item.title: Aria Pro II SB-1000 Left-Handed Bass 1980 - High Gloss — $2,788.50
item.description:
$2,788.50 · Good · EO

Aria Pro II SB-1000 Left-Handed Bass (1980, Made in Japan) – Exceptionally Rare, Upgraded & Gigged

Up for sale is an exceptionally rare left-handed Aria Pro II SB-1000, built in Japan in 1980. Left-handed SB-1000s from this era very rarely come onto the market, making this a fantastic opportunity for collectors and players alike.

I bought this bass in London in 1991 and it became my main gigging instrument throughout the decade. It's been played extensively on stage and has served me incredibly well. Over the last 20 years it has seen very little use, so I've decided it's time for someone else to enjoy it.

Before listing it for sale, I took it to a respected London guitar technician for a full setup and some well-deserved TLC. Interestingly, he had worked as an Aria sales representative during the 1980s and was able to share some fascinating history about these early Japanese SB-1000 models and their reputation for exceptional build quality.

This bass had been significantly upgraded prior to my original purchase with high-quality components, including:

  • Roller bridge
  • Brass nut
  • Schaller machine heads
  • Hand-made pickups
  • Active electronics
  • Hand-built custom circuit board

These upgrades have created a unique instrument that retains the legendary feel and construction of the original Japanese SB-1000 while offering improved tuning stability, reliability and a powerful, versatile sound.

Condition is very good for a bass that's over 45 years old. It naturally shows signs of a life well played, with various dings, scratches and general cosmetic wear consistent with regular gigging, but nothing that affects its playability or performance. It's been professionally set up and is ready to be played immediately.

If you're looking for an authentic vintage Japanese Aria with genuine history, outstanding upgrades and the added rarity of being left-handed, this is an instrument that is unlikely to come along again anytime soon.

Please study the photographs carefully as they form part of the description, and feel free to get in touch if you have any questions or would like any additional photos or videos.

Serial number: 001077

Scale Length: 34" (864 mm)

Nut Width: Approx. 45 mm

Weight: Approx. 4.9 kg (11.0 lbs

item.title: Jackson JS Series JS3Q Kelly Bird IV Bass with Amaranth Fretboard 2019 - Present - Transparent Blue Burst — $296
item.description:
$296 · Very Good · mike's Gear Outlet

don't play it. mama says it's time to go.

item.title: Hohner B Bass - Natural — $475
item.description:
$475 · Very Good · Bond Street Guitars

This 5-string bass features a solid maple body, maple neck, and rosewood fingerboard, equipped with an active preamp for versatile tone shaping. Known for its reliable playability, punchy tone, and solid construction, the B Bass 5-String is a dependable instrument

item.title: Ibanez AEB30-LG Acoustic Bass — $399
item.description:
$399 · Good · Bond Street Guitars

The AEB30 features a AEF shaped body with a soft venetian cutaway. The top is spruce with black and white binding on mahogany back and sides. The round soundhole has an abalone ring rosette. It has a satin-finish mahogany neck with a 22-fret rosewood fingerboard with abalone dot position markers and a matching headstock. Components include a rosewood bridge with pearl dot inlays, an ivorex saddle, a plastic nut, and chrome die-cast tuning machines. Electronics consist of a Fishman sonicore pickup with a AEQ-SS shape shifter preamp with anti-feedback system.

Electronics Tested and working albeit a bit scratchy. Tuning pegs all working fine despite sitting a bit wonky. Plays great!

item.title: Spector Euro 4 LX 2018 - White Gloss — $2,199
item.description:
$2,199 · Very Good · Bond Street Guitars

Sporting the pro features that have made Spector basses coveted by top bassists worldwide for over four decades, the Spector Euro 4 Classic electric bass gives you a comfortable, contoured body carved from solid maple for a bright, deep, punchy, and authoritative tone. The Euro 4 Classic’s fast 3-piece maple neck runs the entire length of the instrument and is topped by a silky smooth 24-fret rosewood fingerboard graced with signature Spector mother-of-pearl crown inlays. Spec’d with a brass locking bridge and brass nut, premium EMG P/J pickups, EMG’s acclaimed BTS preamp system, and elegant gold hardware, the Euro 4 Classic is a high-performance instrument that delivers the tone, playability, versatility, and wicked good looks today’s bass players demand.

item.title: Maruszczyk Jake 32" Fretless P Alder/Rosewood — $1,555.55
item.description:
$1,555.55 · Excellent · Adam's Gear Garage

This is a medium scale fretless P bass that I had custom made with several upgrades from the base model. It's the lightest bass I've ever owned, and the loudest passive bass I've owned (the Aguilar Hot pickup outputs like an active bass). I encourage you to watch the embedded video and zoom in on all the pictures. I didn't find any noteworthy scrapes/dents/defects but please also look through the images yourself for a second opinion. Here are the full specs:

Medium scale 32"

Tone chambered (reduced weight!) alder body

East Indian rosewood fingerboard

21 "frets" (small ghost fret lines)

Hard rock maple neck with carbon rods

Aguilar AG 4P-Hot pickup

Hipshot bridge (17.5mm string spacing) and ultralite tuners with drop-D tuner

Dunlop flush mount strap lock

Thomastik-Infeld Jazz flatwound strings

Nylon gig bag included

item.title: Fender Jino Signature Jazz Bass 2021 - 2022 - Seafoam Green — $1,499
item.description:
$1,499 · Excellent · LB Bass Wrx.

Amazing Jino bass ships safe, securely with gig bag, original pickguard and knobs.

item.title: Cort Bass Guitar 1985-1986 — $300
item.description:
$300 · Very Good · Cross Connection Music & More

This is a Cort bass guitar circa mid-1980s (85-86). I purchased it from the original owner in 1988 and over the years have done a few upgrades. I had the action lowered by a professional luthier, and I also replaced the hardware to accent the gold. It has Wilkinson tuners and bridge. It looks great and sounds even better. The lowered action allows smooth transitions up and down the neck. This bass also comes with gold Schaller strap locks, and a Road Runner padded gig bag.

item.title: Sugi Night Breeze 5 string 2025 — $3,985
item.description:
$3,985 · Excellent · My Gear Outlet

Purchased new December 2025. These are made to order and this one was made for Geek In Box Japan


Specs:

Burl maple top

Korina back

Maple neck with rosewood board

24 frets

35” scale

18mm spacing

19.68” radius (500mm)

Dual humbuckers with individual coil tap switches (humbuckers are not very common on these)

Passive electronics

gotoh hardware custom made for Sugi

9lbs even

Sugi gig bag

item.title: Mayones Hadrien Feraud Signature Jabba HF 5 2020 - Present - Antique Brown — $2,899
item.description:
$2,899 · Excellent · Aidan!

This bass guitar has zero signs of wear or use. this is more than likely brand new and unused. this item obviously comes with the case as well. the lock on the case was removed carefully in order to open and inspect the product. this guitar will be shipped in secure, safe packaging to ensure a safe delivery.

item.title: 2002 Squier Standard Precision Bass Special P/J – 3-Color Sunburst – Hot-Rodded & Luthier Setup (8 lbs 10 oz) — $499
item.description:
$499 · Very Good · Eric's Surplus Gear

Don't let the headstock badge fool you—this 2002 Squier Standard Precision Bass Special in classic 3-Color Sunburst is a serious, hot-rodded player's instrument that punches well above its weight class. Weighing in at a very comfortable 8 lbs, 10 oz, it combines a fast, slim Jazz Bass neck profile with a versatile P/J pickup layout that lets you seamlessly blend tones for any session.

Outfitted with professional hardware upgrades and custom luthier fretwork, this bass delivers incredible punch, sustain, and effortless, low-action playability. Whether you need a reliable, hum-free recording studio workhorse that cuts through a mix or an inspiring, lightweight daily player, you would be hard-pressed to find a better-playing or sounding instrument at this price point.

Upgrades & Custom Modifications

This bass has been thoughtfully upgraded with top-tier components to maximize sustain, tuning stability, and tonal range:

  • Bridge Pickup (J-Position): Upgraded to a Seymour Duncan STK-J2 Classic Stack Jazz Bass pickup. Delivers authentic, punchy J-Bass growl and articulation with zero 60-cycle hum.
  • Middle Pickup (P-Position): Upgraded to a Squier Classic Vibe '70s Split-Coil P-Bass pickup for warm, rich, vintage-voiced fundamental thump.
  • Hardware / Bridge Upgrade: Fitted with a genuine Fender American Standard Bridge, offering superior mass, rock-solid saddle stability, and string-through-body routing for increased acoustic resonance and sustain.
  • Luthier Fretwork: The frets received a professional precision fret level, crown, and dress by independent luthier Shawn May, allowing for exceptionally clean, low action across the entire neck.

Factory & Build Specifications

  • Series: Squier Standard Series (Made in Indonesia)
  • Model: Precision Bass Special (P/J Configuration)
  • Year: 2002
  • Finish: 3-Color Sunburst (Gloss)
  • Body Wood: Agathis (Warm, highly resonant, and lightweight)
  • Neck: Maple, fast-playing "C" profile with a comfortable 1.5" Jazz Bass nut width
  • Fretboard: Rosewood, 9.5" Radius, 20 Frets
  • Controls: Volume (P Pickup), Volume (J Pickup), Master Tone — allows for smooth, infinite tonal blending between both pickups rather than a simple toggle switch.
  • Weight: 8 lbs, 10 oz (Light and easy on the shoulder for long sessions)

Workbench Inspection & Setup

Prior to listing, this instrument underwent a thorough workbench inspection at Eric's Gear so you can plug in and play immediately upon unpacking:

  • Frets: The leveled frets have been polished to a mirror shine for smooth string bending and zero grit.
  • Strings: Currently strung with a very cool set of NOS (New Old Stock) early-2000s Fender 8250 Nickel Roundwounds (.045–.110) on a round core with a tapered E string. They offer fantastic suppleness and rich fundamental punch (very similar in tone and feel to modern DR Pure Blues).
  • Intonation & Stability: Intonates accurately across the entire neck. Tuning stability is rock-solid, and the truss rod adjusts smoothly and reliably in both directions.

Condition & Full Disclosure Report

  • Overall Structural Condition: Excellent. The neck is straight, tuning machines hold pitch tightly, and all electronics function flawlessly with clean, scratch-free pots and a snug output jack.
  • Cosmetics & General Wear: The 3-Color Sunburst finish presents beautifully, showing normal, light play wear consistent with a well-cared-for 2002 instrument (e.g., minor surface swirls and light surface scratches from normal use).
  • Full Disclosure on String-Thru Modification: When upgrading to the Fender American Standard bridge, string-through mounting holes were drilled through the body. While the bridge installation itself is rock-solid, structurally sound, and intonates perfectly, the drilling and ferrule placement on the back of the body show cosmetic DIY imperfections and slight misalignment (please inspect the close-up photos of the rear body). This is strictly a visual flaw on the back of the instrument and has zero impact on structural integrity, tuning stability, or playability.

What’s Included & Shipping

  • Includes: Bass and padded gig bag (Will be padded heavily and packed with extreme care).
  • Shipping: Packed securely in a heavy-duty shipping box. Orders typically ship within 1 business day via UPS Ground or FedEx Home Delivery with full tracking and insurance provided.

item.title: ACG SLG Short Scale Passive Tonestyler — $2,400
item.description:
$2,400 · Excellent · Scott's Shop

This is a short scale ACG SLG passive bass with a 10 way tonestyler know. Battery compartment is only their if you decide to add active electronics. Black Limba body with a crazy figured silverberry top. Possibly my favorite short scale bass ever. Ebony fretboard and headstock face. Comes with a padded gig bag and weighs in at 8.95 lbs. See photos and ask questions if you have them. Feel free to check out my other listings as well.

item.title: Gibson Thunderbird IV 2013 - Vintage Sunburst — $2,499
item.description:
$2,499 · Good · Bond Street Guitars

The Gibson Thunderbird has the classic reverse body and headstock design as originally introduced in 1963 as Gibson's first neck-through-body bass design. The traditional 9-ply mahogany/walnut neck through body construction provides a thundering low end response and a piano like sustain. The narrow nut width and rounded neck profile neck feels both fast and intuitive. The Thunderbird high output, ceramic magnet loaded humbucking pickups (neck and bridge) deliver the sonic and iconic low end voice for which the Thunderbird is known.

This bass has been worked on by its previous owner and has some trouble with bridge adjustment as they clearly went after it pretty crudely but after some work from our techs it's playing and feeling great.
